channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Pastor’s White House prayer claims Iranians seek death of all Jews
item.description: Evangelical pastor Franklin Graham claimed Iranians seek to kill Jews worldwide and said Trump was chosen by God.

item.title: Argentina expels Iranian diplomat amid rift over blacklisting IRGC
item.description: Buenos Aires accuses Tehran of making 'offensive' accusations against Argentina after it labeled IRGC as 'terror' group.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Iran and Infantino talks progressing as war darkens US trip
item.description: Iranian football officials hold first face-to-face meeting with FIFA's president since start of US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: US-Israeli strike hits newly opened B1 bridge near Tehran, killing two
item.description: A US-Israeli strike targeted the B1 bridge in Karaj, west of Tehran, killing at least two people.

item.title: US reinforcements are coming to the Gulf – but what does victory look like?
item.description: Al Jazeera's defence editor looks at how the US-Israeli attack on Iran has become a regional war

item.title: Can the US seize Iran’s enriched uranium – and what are the risks?
item.description: A military operation would pose chemical, logistical, and tactical hurdles, experts say.

item.title: Iran releases mocking missile launch video after Trump threats
item.description: Iranian state media released footage showing the launch of missiles plastered with messages mocking Donald Trump.

item.title: CENTCOM releases video of air strikes in fifth week of war on Iran
item.description: US Central Command has released footage of strikes in Iran appearing to show the targeting of drones and tanks.

item.title: Can Starmer’s 40-nation coalition open the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: About 20 percent of global oil and gas passes through the Gulf waterway.

item.title: Burkina Faso military, allies committing ‘horrific’ civilian abuses: HRW
item.description: More than 1,800 civilians have been killed by the army and armed groups since 2023, Human Rights Watch says.
